text:
The subject of this paper is the assessment of attitudes, behavior and expectations of middle-aged Poles towards agritourism as a form of recreational activity. The development of agritourism farms in Poland is progressing rapidly, but there is a considerable surplus of tourism products on the market in relation to the number of visitors. A survey carried out in 2015 among 326 respondents made it possible to determine the barriers blocking the participation of non-users in the agritourism activity, as well as to assess the attitudes of agritourism users. It has been established that non-users are characterized by low awareness of agritourism and rural tourism and also by the stereotype that the countryside has nothing to offer. On the other hand, health, peace and quietness are the basic motives for choosing agritourism for users.

A survey of 360 middle-aged holidaymakers showed that while price was a factor, it was also very important to have adequate accommodation standards, as well as a restaurant on site. The most important sources for choosing a holiday site were the internet and personal recommendations. The tourists also showed a strong interest for adventure activities, such as cycling and walking, as well as cultural activities such as local food tastings and folk dancing. According to the article, the rural tourism development should concentrate on improving the infrastructure and accommodation sanitation and to find new marketing ways to fight the bad image of rural areas.
